meta.notes: v1.2: LinkedIn on Tue/Thu only (avoids Flow OS posts on Mon/Wed/Fri). Instagram Mon/Wed/Fri. Facebook Wed/Fri.

monthly_themes.description: Introduce the project, explain the three pillars, drive EOI registrations
Behind the scenes of finding the right land in Crete. Soil, water, climate, village proximity.

content_slots.brief: Introduce the project. Three pillars: agricultural land, village restoration, health and wellness. One ecosystem in Crete. The land feeds the village, the village houses the people, the wellness centre supports their health.
content_slots.cta: Link in bio to register your interest at creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: Professional introduction to the Crete Projects opportunity. Three interconnected ventures under one ecosystem. Position it as a tangible alternative asset with lifestyle alignment. Mention the EOI process. Speak to investors and entrepreneurs.

Why Crete specifically. Year-round Mediterranean climate, affordable land compared to mainland Europe, deep cultural heritage of village life and food, growing interest from digital nomads and travelling families.

Introduce the project to a community-oriented audience. Emphasise the worldschooling angle: premium accommodation for travelling families, agricultural workshops for kids, community spaces. Not a commune, not a hostel. Private, premium, curated.

The investment case for regenerative agriculture in Crete. Multiple revenue streams from one asset: produce sales, agro-tourism, workshop fees, member access. Biodynamic principles building soil health and long-term land value.
content_slots.cta: Expression of Interest open at creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: The agricultural pillar explained. Regenerative agroforestry and biodynamic principles. Diversified produce: olives, herbs, vegetables, citrus, honey, livestock. Farm-to-table supply chain. This is a working farm, not a decorative garden.
The ecosystem explained simply. The land grows the food. The village houses the people. The wellness centre supports their health. Three diversified revenue streams that reinforce each other.

Village restoration explained. Traditional Cretan stone buildings transformed into premium private accommodation. Thick stone walls, courtyards, arched doorways, timber beams. Heritage architecture with contemporary comfort.

The practitioner model. Wellness practitioners come to the facility, use the space and the built-in audience, and pay a facility fee. Quality and range of services scale without employing a full clinical team. Write about this as a business model.

The wellness facility. Gym, cold plunge, sauna, physiotherapy, sports recovery. Yoga, pilates, breathwork, movement classes. Visiting and resident practitioners. This is what turns accommodation into a destination.

What does a worldschooling hub look like at Crete Projects? Groups of 10-30 families staying 2-8 weeks. Private accommodation, dedicated learning spaces, agricultural immersion programmes, wellness facility access.
content_slots.cta: If you run a worldschooling hub, we'd like to hear from you. creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: What we're NOT building. Not a commune, co-living collective, shared dormitory, budget destination, or volunteer exchange. Private and premium accommodation. Community curated through investment and membership, not self-selection.

The founder story. 20 years in the building industry before pivoting to health, wellness, and mentoring. Wife Emma with a long history in health and wellness, now business coaching. Two lenses on this project: build quality and human experience.
content_slots.cta: Read the full EOI at creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: Month-end recap. Foundation phase progress: EOI campaign running, legal entity in progress, land and property sourcing underway. Next month: land sourcing deep dives. Real updates, not manufactured excitement.
content_slots.cta: Follow along and register at creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: What we look for in agricultural land in Crete. Soil quality, water access, sun exposure, proximity to a village, road access, existing olive groves or orchards. Walk through the actual criteria.
Agricultural land acquisition in Greece: what you need to know. Legal considerations for foreign buyers, the process of purchasing agricultural land, due diligence requirements, zoning and planning.
Crete's growing seasons. What you can grow and when. Olive harvest in autumn, citrus in winter, herbs and vegetables year-round, honey production cycles.
Regenerative agroforestry explained. What it means in practice: no monoculture, diverse planting systems, building soil health, integrating animals, working with natural cycles.

Due diligence on agricultural land in Crete. What we're checking: land registry (cadastre) status, zoning, access rights, water rights, environmental protections, proximity to infrastructure. The unsexy but critical work that protects investor capital.
content_slots.cta: Expression of Interest open at creteprojects.com

content_slots.brief: Water in Crete. How water access works for agricultural land: bore holes, natural springs, seasonal rainfall, irrigation systems. Why water is the single most important factor in land selection.

What a day on the Crete Projects farm could look like. Morning harvest, workshop with visiting families, produce prep for the village kitchen, afternoon maintenance. A realistic picture, not a fantasy.
content_slots.cta: Be part of it at creteprojects.com
